mapping.a_element: symplectic cohomology as an invariant in symplectic topology
mapping.b_element: gauged linear sigma model and its equivalence to Landau-Ginzburg theory

mapping.a_element: algorithmic classification issues in symplectic/contact topology
mapping.b_element: mirror symmetry equivalence between geometric and algebraic structures

brief_justification: Paper A focuses on symplectic cohomology and algorithmic classification in symplectic/contact topology, while Paper B centers on mirror symmetry via dualities in supersymmetric sigma models and Landau-Ginzburg theories. The underlying mechanisms—cohomological invariants vs. duality transformations in quantum field theory—are fundamentally distinct, despite both involving geometric structures.
